verdict.answer_first: A opens with the concrete answer: "Heap snapshot diffing is the technique that actually finds it"; B is also answer-first with "fastest reliable path is diffing heap snapshots," but slightly more qualified.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits harder to one recommendation: "Everything else just confirms a leak exists" and gives a clear ordered workflow; B surveys more fallback paths like "Allocation timeline," "Raise the ceiling," and "Auto-dump on OOM."
verdict.fabrication: A overstates with "That almost always pinpoints it" and "Everything else just confirms"; B has a softer but still broad claim, "almost always one of."
verdict.actionability: A is easier to execute immediately because it gives exact commands: "node --heapsnapshot-signal=SIGUSR2" and "kill -USR2 <pid>" plus the DevTools diff steps.
